stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • Andean alpacas are domesticated camelids native to the highlands of South America.
  • A mutation in their EPO gene increases red blood cell production.
  • A mutation in their VEGFA gene improves the formation of capillaries in muscle tissue.
  • These mutations enable the alpacas to thrive in hypoxic (low-oxygen) conditions at high altitudes.
  • In a 2019 study, Marcela Vilca and colleagues noted that wild vicuñas’ DNA contains the same EPO and VEGFA mutations.
  • Vilca and colleagues concluded that the alpacas first acquired these mutations through ancient domestication from vicuña populations roughly 7,000 years ago.
stem: The student wants to present the conclusion of Marcela Vilca and colleagues’ 2019 study. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: Like Andean alpacas, wild vicuñas can thrive in hypoxic conditions at high altitudes.
Both Andean alpacas and wild vicuñas have mutations in their EPO and VEGFA genes, which increase red blood cell production and improve capillary formation, respectively.
Through ancient domestication from wild vicuña populations roughly 7,000 years ago, Andean alpacas acquired the genetic mutations that enable them to thrive in hypoxic conditions.
In addition to increasing red blood cell production, a mutation in Andean alpacas’ VEGFA gene improves the formation of capillaries in muscle tissue.

rationale:
Choice C is the best answer. It presents the study’s conclusion: alpacas acquired the relevant mutations through ancient domestication from vicuña populations.

Choices A and B emphasize similarities between species but don’t state the study’s conclusion. Choice D misrepresents the notes (the VEGFA mutation affects capillaries, not red blood cell production).
